sections.paragraph:
in Kal part. כֹּזֵב Psa 116:11 of more frequent occurrence in-

Piel כִּזֵּב to lie, Job 6:28 34:6 Pro 14:5. (Arab. كذب.) Followed by לְ to lie to any one, to deceive him. Psa 78:36 89:36 , “shall I lie unto David?” i.e. break my fidelity (comp. Num 23:19). Eze 13:19, followed by בְּ id. 2Ki 4:16. Metaph. applied to water quickly drying up and disappointing the traveller, comp. אַכְזָב.

Hiphil, to reprove of lying, to convict of falsehood, Job 24:25.

Niphal, pass. of Hiphil, to be proved false, or deceitful, Job 41:1.

The derivatives follow, except אַכְזָב, אַכְזִיב.
